questionnaire.total-estimated-net-worth-usd-help: This is the value that is left after subtracting liabilities from assets. Assets include checking and savings account balances, the value of securities such as stocks or bonds, real property value, the market value of an automobile, etc. Examples of liabilities include debts like mortgages, credit card balances, student loans, and car loans. Liabilities can also include obligations that must be paid such as bills and taxes.

trading-account-overview.changeLeverageWarning: You currently have open positions. To change the leverage size, please close all open positions first.
trading-account-overview.equity-tooltip: The net balance of your account. Your deposit minus any losses and plus any profits.
trading-account-overview.floatingPnl-tooltip: The net financial outcome of a trading position over a certain period.
trading-account-overview.freeMargin-tooltip: The equity in your trading account not tied up in open positions.
trading-account-overview.marginLevel-tooltip:
The percentage of your equity vs the used margin. In other words, it shows how healthy your account is (the bigger the number, the better).

Margin level is calculated using the formula:
Margin level = (equity / used margin) x 100
trading-account-overview.maxLeverage-tooltip:
The use of borrowed capital in order to open a trade.

For example, if you are trading with 1:100 leverage, you would only need $10 to open a trade worth $1000 (10x100=1000).
trading-account-overview.reservedMargin-tooltip:
The maximum extra margin needed to cover any open orders that may be filled.

If there are no open orders, the reserved margin is 0.
trading-account-overview.server-name-tooltip: To use the server name in MetaTrader, enter it during the platform’s login process. It connects you to the broker’s trading server, enabling trade execution and access to market data.
trading-account-overview.title: Trading account

verification.question-mark-text: Identity Verification is essential for complying with regulatory requirements and keeping your account safe. By verifying your identity, you are protecting yourself from potential fraud or identity theft. It is also an important step to prevent illegal activities such as money laundering and ensuring that your funds and personal information are secure.
